records.ety: [Cf. Dan. kaste, Icel. & Sw. kasta; perh. akin to L. gerere to bear, carry. E. jest.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Cast; p. pr. & vb. n. Casting.]

defs: To send or drive by force; to throw; to fling; to hurl; to impel.
quotes: Uzziah prepared . . . slings to cast stones.
Cast thy garment about thee, and follow me.
We must be cast upon a certain island.

defs: To direct or turn, as the eyes.
quotes: How earnestly he cast his eyes upon me!

defs: To drop; to deposit; as, to cast a ballot.
To throw down, as in wrestling.
To throw up, as a mound, or rampart.
quotes: Thine enemies shall cast a trench [bank] about thee.

defs: To throw off; to eject; to shed; to lose.
quotes: His filth within being cast.
Neither shall your vine cast her fruit.
The creatures that cast the skin are the snake, the viper, etc.

defs: To bring forth prematurely; to slink.
quotes: Thy she-goats have not cast their young.

defs: To throw out or emit; to exhale.
quotes: This . . . casts a sulphureous smell.

defs: To cause to fall; to shed; to reflect; to throw; as, to cast a ray upon a screen; to cast light upon a subject.
To impose; to bestow; to rest.
quotes: The government I cast upon my brother.
Cast thy burden upon the Lord.

defs: To dismiss; to discard; to cashier.
quotes: The state can not with safety cast him.

defs: To compute; to reckon; to calculate; as, to cast a horoscope.
quotes: You cast the event of war, my noble lord.

defs: To contrive; to plan.
quotes: The cloister . . . had, I doubt not, been cast for [an orange-house].

defs: To defeat in a lawsuit; to decide against; to convict; as, to be cast in damages.
quotes: She was cast to be hanged.
Were the case referred to any competent judge, they would inevitably be cast.

defs: To turn (the balance or scale); to overbalance; hence, to make preponderate; to decide; as, a casting voice.
quotes: How much interest casts the balance in cases dubious!

defs: To form into a particular shape, by pouring liquid metal or other material into a mold; to fashion; to found; as, to cast bells, stoves, bullets.
To stereotype or electrotype.
To fix, distribute, or allot, as the parts of a play among actors; also to assign (an actor) for a part.
quotes: Our parts in the other world will be new cast.

defs: To throw, as a line in angling, esp, with a fly hook.
To turn the head of a vessel around from the wind in getting under weigh.
quotes: Weigh anchor, cast to starboard.

defs: To consider; to turn or revolve in the mind; to plan; as, to cast about for reasons.
quotes: She . . . cast in her mind what manner of salution this should be.

defs: To calculate; to compute.
quotes: Who would cast and balance at a desk.

defs: To receive form or shape in a mold.
quotes: It will not run thin, so as to cast and mold.

defs: To warp; to become twisted out of shape.
quotes: Stuff is said to cast or warp when . . . it alters its flatness or straightness.

defs: To vomit.
quotes: These verses . . . make me ready to cast.

defs: 3d pers. pres. of Cast, for Casteth.
The act of casting or throwing; a throw.
The thing thrown.
quotes: A cast of dreadful dust.

defs: The distance to which a thing is or can be thrown.
A throw of dice; hence, a chance or venture.
quotes: An even cast whether the army should march this way or that way.
I have set my life upon a cast, And I will stand the hazard of the die.

defs: That which is throw out or off, shed, or ejected; as, the skin of an insect, the refuse from a hawk's stomach, the excrement of a earthworm.
The act of casting in a mold.
quotes: And why such daily cast of brazen cannon.

defs: An impression or mold, taken from a thing or person; amold; a pattern.
That which is formed in a mild; esp. a reproduction or copy, as of a work of art, in bronze or plaster, etc.; a casting.
Form; appearance; mien; air; style; as, a peculiar cast of countenance.
quotes: An heroic poem, but in another cast and figure.
And thus the native hue of resolution Is sicklied o'er with the pale cast of thought.

defs: A tendency to any color; a tinge; a shade.
quotes: Gray with a cast of green.

defs: A chance, opportunity, privilege, or advantage; specifically, an opportunity of riding; a lift.
quotes: We bargained with the driver to give us a cast to the next stage.
If we had the cast o' a cart to bring it.

defs: The assignment of parts in a play to the actors.
A flight or a couple or set of hawks let go at one time from the hand.
quotes: As when a cast of falcons make their flight.

defs: A stoke, touch, or trick.
quotes: This was a cast of Wood's politics; for his information was wholly false.

defs: A motion or turn, as of the eye; direction; look; glance; squint.
quotes: The cast of the eye is a gesture of aversion.
And let you see with one cast of an eye.
This freakish, elvish cast came into the child's eye.

defs: A tube or funnel for conveying metal into a mold.
Four; that is, as many as are thrown into a vessel at once in counting herrings, etc; a warp.
Contrivance; plot, design.
